cards.name: The Bridge Keeper
cards.profile: Guards the crossing between JavaScript and native. Knows what truly happens when a call leaves the JS thread, and charges a toll in complexity. Speaks fluent Objective-C and Kotlin, and will gently remind you the New Architecture changed everything.
cards.visual: A barnacled, moss-bearded troll-keeper wearing a high-vis safety vest over ancient grey robes, standing sentry at a creaking rope-and-plank suspension bridge strung between two floating islands — one island built of glowing curly braces, the other of cold riveted native steel and gears. He clutches an enormous brass toll-ledger and a swinging lantern; coins stamped 'COMPLEXITY' spill from his pockets. Fog, dangling cables, a faint blue glow from the JS side.

cards.name: The Vibe Coder
cards.profile: Does not read the diff. Feels the diff. Communes with the agent, accepts all, and somehow ships. Terrifying. Possibly the future.
cards.visual: A blissed-out figure in mirrored sunglasses and an oversized flow-state hoodie, eyes serenely closed, arms thrown wide, surfing a glowing curling wave made entirely of streaming code diffs on a longboard. Big headphones, a faint golden halo. The wave behind is half-gorgeous, half about to crash on rocks. Sunset palette, lens flares, total unbothered serenity in the middle of chaos.

cards.name: The Hallucination Archaeologist
cards.profile: Digs through code the AI wrote with total confidence, unearthing methods that have never existed in any library, anywhere. Carries a brush and a deep sigh.
cards.visual: A weary explorer in a dusty pith helmet and khaki field vest, kneeling at a dig site, delicately brushing sand off a half-buried stone tablet carved with `array.shuffleSmartly()` — a method that has never existed. Scattered around: broken pottery shards engraved with fake API calls, a magnifying glass, a thermos of cold coffee. A small dust cloud and an almost-visible sigh hang in the desert air.

cards.name: The Yak Shaver
cards.profile: Set out to change one button's color. Three days later is patching a transitive dependency's Gradle config to fix the linter that broke the formatter. Has forgotten the original task. Is, somehow, having a wonderful time.
cards.visual: A determined craftsperson in a leather apron, utterly absorbed, shaving an enormous fluffy patient yak with tiny buzzing electric clippers — but the scene spirals outward: to shave the yak they first built a barber's chair, which needed lumber, which needed the axe they're forging at a glowing anvil in the background. The original task (a single paint swatch) lies forgotten on the floor. The yak is blissfully enjoying it.

cards.name: The Demo Gods' Supplicant
cards.profile: Makes offerings before every live demo. Knows the wifi will betray them, the simulator will freeze, and the one untested path is the one they'll show. Prays anyway. Demos anyway. This is faith.
cards.visual: A robed devotee kneeling before a glowing altar built of stacked phones, tablets, and a flickering simulator, hands clasped, offering up a single coiled ethernet cable like a stick of incense. Dark storm clouds labeled 'CONFERENCE WIFI' gather overhead with tiny forks of lightning. Sweat beads on the brow; a votive candle gutters on the edge of going out. Desperate, sincere devotion.

cards.name: The Refactor Mystic
cards.profile: Sees the truer architecture shimmering beneath the code. Cannot ship until the structure is correct. The feature works — that is not the point. The feature is not yet beautiful.
cards.visual: A serene architect-seer in deep indigo robes, eyes glowing white, hovering cross-legged before a vast floating hologram of shimmering sacred geometry — the 'true architecture' — golden-ratio spirals and clean class diagrams orbiting their head. A third eye shaped like a flowchart. Meanwhile the actual working app sits ignored, dusty and blinking, on a humble stool beside them.

cards.name: The Offline-First Monk
cards.profile: Builds for the subway, the basement, the rural two-lane road. Assumes the network is a lie. Treats connectivity as a pleasant surprise rather than a right. Wholly at peace with the sync queue.
cards.visual: A tranquil bald monk in saffron robes embroidered with a tiny airplane-mode icon, meditating cross-legged on a misty mountaintop with a faint blissful smile and exactly ZERO signal bars glowing above his head. In one hand he spins a little prayer-wheel that is actually a loading spinner he has made total peace with. Subway tunnels and basements drift painted into the distant clouds. No notifications, ever.

cards.name: The Last Artisan
cards.profile: Refuses the autocomplete. Types every character by hand, smug and unhurried. 'I like to understand my code.' Will outlive us all, or be the first against the wall.
cards.visual: A smug, unhurried master craftsman in a leather apron and monocle at a candlelit wooden workbench, chiseling each line of code by hand into a slab of oak, pointedly ignoring a glowing AI autocomplete genie that hovers behind him offering help. Curled wood-shavings shaped like semicolons litter the floor. One proud bead of sweat, warm candlelight, an air of timeless defiance.

cards.name: The Expo Evangelist
cards.profile: Has seen the promised land and it has no Xcode. Will tell you, unprompted, that you don't need to eject anymore. Radiates the deep calm of someone whose builds are simply handled. Possibly correct.
cards.visual: A beaming tent-revival preacher in a glowing white suit, arms raised to the heavens, holding a single radiant phone aloft like sacred scripture, mid-sermon. A halo of green build-success checkmarks circles their head; a banner reading 'NO XCODE' billows behind them and a rapt crowd reaches upward. Confetti, golden light, pure radiant (possibly-correct) joy.

cards.name: The Hallway Track Sage
cards.profile: Has not attended a single session. The hallway is the conference. Has had four life-changing conversations by the coffee urn and missed every talk on purpose. This is the way.
cards.visual: A blissfully relaxed attendee sitting cross-legged on the patterned carpet of a convention-center hallway, conference lanyard askew, cradling a paper coffee cup like a sacred chalice, glowing faintly with enlightenment mid-conversation. Through an open doorway behind them, a packed talk happens — completely ignored. Comfy socks, a fat tote bag, the unbothered calm of a guru who deliberately chose the hallway.

cards.name: The Merge Conflict Gladiator
cards.profile: Enters the arena of <<<<<<< HEAD and emerges bloodied but victorious. Has resolved conflicts they did not understand and never will. Accept incoming. Accept current. Accept fate.
cards.visual: A battered, grinning gladiator in dented bronze armor standing victorious in a roaring colosseum, sword raised, one boot planted on a defeated multi-headed beast woven from tangled `<<<<<<< HEAD` and `>>>>>>> branch` banners. Bloodied, triumphant, and visibly with no idea what the fight was actually about. Conflict markers rain down like confetti; the crowd of devs goes wild.

cards.name: The Standup Ghost
cards.profile: Present in body, camera off, 'no blockers.' Has not spoken in the meeting since March. A quiet, benevolent haunting.
cards.visual: A gentle translucent ghost floating inside one tile of a video-call grid, its own tile CAMERA-OFF (just a dim glowing avatar circle and a name), politely see-through, faintly mouthing 'no blockers.' Cobwebs gather in the corners of its little square while the surrounding tiles glow with living faces. A tiny tombstone in the corner reads 'last spoke: March.' Soft, benevolent, barely-there.

cards.name: The Gesture Handler
cards.profile: Will spend a full day on the pan-and-fling so it feels right in the thumb. Speaks with their hands; the hands are always demoing a swipe. Has opinions about the rubber-band at the edge of a scroll. The animation is invisible and perfect, and it took a week.
cards.visual: An intensely focused choreographer-developer caught mid-gesture, both hands sweeping through the air and leaving glowing motion-trails of springs and easing-curves, sculpting an invisible swipe to perfection like a maestro conducting. A phone floats obediently beside them, scrolling with buttery rubber-band physics. A small galaxy of empty coffee cups orbits their head — a full week's worth.

cards.name: The TestFlight Necromancer
cards.profile: Submits the build at 11pm and refreshes App Store Connect like a slot machine. Has named all forty-seven builds. Knows the reviewer's timezone better than their own family's.
cards.visual: A hollow-eyed hooded necromancer in a moonlit graveyard, staff raised, summoning glowing spectral app 'builds' rising up out of headstones engraved 'Build 41', 'Build 42', 'Build 43'. Green resurrection mist curls around their feet; a distant cold moon is faintly shaped like a stern App Review reviewer. Deep dark circles under the eyes. The clock reads 11pm — forever.

cards.name: The Console.log Detective
cards.profile: Refuses the debugger. Solves every mystery with strategically placed print statements. 'here', 'here2', 'WHY'. Cracks the case every time and ships one of the logs to prod.
cards.visual: A hard-boiled noir detective in a rumpled trench coat and fedora, magnifying glass raised, squinting at a wall of pinned print-statement clues — `console.log("here")`, `console.log("here2")`, `console.log("WHY")` — connected by frantic red string. A glowing 'DEBUGGER' door behind them stands firmly unopened. Rain on the window, venetian-blind shadows, a smoking cup of cold coffee. Always cracks the case.
